# `liber:process@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:process` package (`process.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem process package - process loading and job control.

## Imports

- `error, koid` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### record `process-info`

One process the ProcessService has started: the kernel object id of the new process and the init-package entry name it was launched from.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `koid` | `koid` |  |
| `name` | `string` |  |

### resource `task`

A live process the launcher drives - the kernel Process object handed back as a capability (carrying MANAGE), so the caller can do job control on the program it started: signal it and wait on it. Named `task` to stay distinct from the `process` service interface; it is the same kernel Process the koid names.

An opaque kernel object, transferred as `handle<task>`.

### record `start-result`

The result of launching a program: the live process as a capability (transferred out-of-band as handle<task>) plus its process-info (koid + name). The handle is the launcher's job-control handle on the started program.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `task` | `handle<task>` |  |
| `info` | `process-info` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `process`

The Process service contract: the process-loading mechanism. `start` launches an unattended program (no bootstrap capability), `list` reports the processes started so far, and `launch` is the full launcher primitive - it loads and starts a program with a caller-provided bootstrap channel (so a policy front end like PermissionManager can grant the new process its capabilities over that channel) and returns the live process handle for job control. `launch-bounded` is the same mechanism under a reusable child Domain keyed by the caller's memory limit; concurrent processes with the same limit share that aggregate budget. The policy front end chooses the limit, while ProcessService remains responsible only for loading. The service holds no grantable clients and decides no capability grants.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `start` | `start(name: string) -> result<process-info, error>` |  |
| 2 | `list` | `list() -> result<list<process-info>, error>` |  |
| 3 | `launch` | `launch(name: string, bootstrap: handle<channel>) -> result<start-result, error>` |  |
| 4 | `launch-bounded` | `launch-bounded(name: string, memory-limit: u64, bootstrap: handle<channel>) -> result<start-result, error>` |  |
